title: platform-user — kill switches
description: Platform-plane kill switches. Every flag defaults to `on` — platform-user is a core dependency and its subsystems must be live in production. Kill switches exist for emergency degradation (a bad deploy of one subsystem should not take the whole platform_admin surface down).

flags.description: Master toggle for the entire platform_admin surface. Off = every /api/v1/platform/* route returns 503 Service Unavailable. Emergency-only — used during a Sev-1 to fully lock out platform staff (rare).

The `platform.audit` middleware. Off = audit rows for platform-plane requests are NOT written. Kill-switch guards against a bug in the middleware causing audit-write failures that would otherwise 5xx every platform-admin request. Compliance is degraded when off; toggle back on within 1h and page compliance officer + DPO.

The `platform.mfa_required` middleware runtime enforcement. Off = MFA is only checked at login (login-time enforcement is separate + not toggle-able here). NEVER turn off in production without an incident commander sign-off + a rollback ETA.

The observer's refusal to create a PlatformUser row when the referenced Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted. Off = allow (used in dev + during migration when historical Identities have not yet enrolled). Toggle back on before opening the environment for platform-admin traffic.

OnCallResolver. Off = every resolver query returns null + `/on-call/current` returns a `RESOLVER_DISABLED` response. Escalation fallback is Slack broadcast to the department channel (out-of-band from this module).

PagerDuty integration. Off = on-call rotation falls back to the internal `on_call_rotation_entries` table + notifications go via Slack instead of PagerDuty. Used when PagerDuty is unreachable or credentials expire.
PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob. Off = offboarded PlatformUsers linger past the 90-day retention hold. Used when compliance requires an extended-retention pause (e.g. active litigation).

RevokePlatformUserAccessJob dispatch on PlatformUserOffboarded. Off = access revocation is manual. Emergency-only — SOC 2 24h SLA becomes at risk when off; compliance-officer sign-off required to enable.
